commands.cmd:
pid=4242
if [ "$pid" -le 0 ]; then
  pat=''
  [ -n "$pat" ] || exit 1
  pid=$(pgrep -f -- "$pat" 2>/dev/null | head -n 1)
  [ -n "$pid" ] || exit 1
fi
s1=$(jstat -gcutil "$pid") || exit 1
sleep 5
s2=$(jstat -gcutil "$pid") || exit 1
out=$(printf '%s\n===\n%s\n' "$s1" "$s2" | awk -v win=5 '
  function val(name,  i){ return $(col[name]) }
  /^===$/ { phase=2; next }
  # Record FGC/GCT presence at HEADER time via a membership test (`in` does
  # not vivify) BEFORE any data-row val() does `$(col[name])` — that deref
  # auto-vivifies the key, so a post-hoc `"FGC" in col` END check would be a
  # dead no-op under mawk (the Debian/Ubuntu default awk). hdr_ok captures
  # the true header state. heap.yaml is immune the same way (gate-before-deref).
  /FGC/ { for (i=1;i<=NF;i++) col[$i]=i; hdr_ok = (("FGC" in col) && ("GCT" in col)); next }
  {
    if (phase==2) { fgc2=val("FGC"); gct2=val("GCT") }
    else          { fgc1=val("FGC"); gct1=val("GCT") }
  }
  END {
    # Fail loud (empty out → exit 1 gate) if the header lacked FGC/GCT
    # (e.g. JDK column drift) rather than diffing field-0 garbage.
    if (!hdr_ok) { exit }
    fgcd = fgc2 - fgc1; if (fgcd < 0) fgcd = 0
    gctd = gct2 - gct1; if (gctd < 0) gctd = 0
    pct = (win > 0 ? gctd/win*100 : 0)
    printf "%d %.1f", fgcd, pct
  }')
fgc_delta=$(printf '%s' "$out" | awk '{print $1}')
gc_time_pct=$(printf '%s' "$out" | awk '{print $2}')
[ -n "$fgc_delta" ] || exit 1
[ -n "$gc_time_pct" ] || exit 1
printf '{"full_gc_delta":%s,"gc_time_pct":%s}' "$fgc_delta" "$gc_time_pct"
